reason: Frontend directory conventions — needed to know where Electron adapters and sidecar status components should live
Component patterns — SidecarStatusPage must follow existing component conventions
State management patterns — need to understand how to surface sidecar status in the React app
TypeScript conventions — Electron main/preload/renderer must follow the project's type safety standards
Quality bar for frontend code — Electron layer must meet the same testing/lint standards
Hook conventions — if we add useSidecarStatus or similar hooks
Backend structure — sidecar spawn logic needs to know where xreadagent.api lives and how it's invoked
Error handling conventions — sidecar crash/restart and error page must follow backend error patterns
Logging patterns — sidecar stdout/stderr capture and log display must follow backend logging conventions
Cross-layer data flow — Electron ↔ sidecar IPC is a new cross-layer boundary
Detailed technical research on Electron+Python sidecar architecture — spawn patterns, IPC design, distribution, code examples
Task PRD — all requirements, decisions, acceptance criteria
